---

Answers to the five scoped questions:

**1. Is the error surface machine-actionable?**

Yes for the six defined ErrorClass constants — they map cleanly to retry (adapter-timeout), abort-and-report (adapter-panic), abort-and-reconfigure (adapter-protocol-mismatch, adapter-capability-lied), and abort-and-fix-trust (adapter-exec-denied, adapter-undeclared-output). The gap is that there is no class for the case where an adapter finds the IR semantically invalid at emit time. That forces the caller to parse the human-readable `Message` string. This is W2 above. The class set is frozen at the wire layer in this PR; PR 3 is the last safe moment to add `adapter-invalid-ir`.

**2. Are OpWarning fields structured for programmatic consumption?**

Partially. `concept_id` (stable string key) and `status` (closed enum `degraded|partial`) give an agent a usable two-field branch surface. The `note` field is prose-only. The design is usable as-is if the PR 3 spec documents that `concept_id + status` is the complete stable branch surface and that `note` MUST NOT be parsed programmatically. If there are v1 warning scenarios where two different reasons produce the same `status` on the same `concept_id`, a `code` sub-classifier field will be needed. This is W1.

**3. Can an adapter author understand how to implement an adapter from this package alone?**

Mostly yes. The package doc in `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/aienvs/internal/adapter/contract/framing.go` (line 1-9) explicitly says the spec doc ships in PR 3 and points there. The JSON Schemas give a formal field contract, and the parity test validates alignment. The one genuine confusion point is O2 — `DeclaredOutput.JSONPath` and `ToolOwnedKindJSONPointer` refer to two different locator standards, and the test fixtures use both inconsistently. An implementer of `write_tool_owned` will be confused about which standard to use in the `declared_outputs` list vs. the op locator field.

**4. Does the contract leak user-facing concerns into the wire layer?**

No. The package is byte-pure. There are no ANSI codes, no color references, no terminal-width logic, and no formatting. `Error.Message` is a plain string (required by JSON-RPC 2.0 spec), and its contents are adapter-supplied and not validated by this layer — which is correct.

**5. Are there agent-blocking concerns hidden in the design?**

None that are blocking. The `isOp()` unexported sentinel means the `DecodeOp` switch is exhaustive over the wire contract and external types cannot accidentally satisfy `Op` — this is safe and correct. All error values carry context beyond their sentinel identity. The `CapabilityLevel` enum is closed and all values are named constants. The only subtle issue is that an agent acting on `adapter-timeout` has no `retryable` field in `ErrorData` — it must apply per-class retry policy from its own knowledge of what `adapter-timeout` means. Given the class set is small, this is acceptable for v1 (O3).

Full analysis saved to `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/aienvs/.context/compound-engineering/ce-review/20260425-032742-85562cea/agent-native.json`.</result>

Mostly yes. The package doc in `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/aienvs/internal/adapter/contract/framing.go` (line 1-9) explicitly says the spec doc ships in PR 3 and points there. The JSON Schemas give a formal field contract, and the parity test validates alignment. The one genuine confusion point is O2 — `DeclaredOutput.JSONPath` and `ToolOwnedKindJSONPointer` refer to two different locator standards, and the test fixtures use both inconsistently. An implementer of `write_tool_owned` will be confused about which standard to use in the `declared_outputs` list vs. the op locator field.

**4. Does the contract leak user-facing concerns into the wire layer?**

No. The package is byte-pure. There are no ANSI codes, no color references, no terminal-width logic, and no formatting. `Error.Message` is a plain string (required by JSON-RPC 2.0 spec), and its contents are adapter-supplied and not validated by this layer — which is correct.

**5. Are there agent-blocking concerns hidden in the design?**

None that are blocking. The `isOp()` unexported sentinel means the `DecodeOp` switch is exhaustive over the wire contract and external types cannot accidentally satisfy `Op` — this is safe and correct. All error values carry context beyond their sentinel identity. The `CapabilityLevel` enum is closed and all values are named constants. The only subtle issue is that an agent acting on `adapter-timeout` has no `retryable` field in `ErrorData` — it must apply per-class retry policy from its own knowledge of what `adapter-timeout` means. Given the class set is small, this is acceptable for v1 (O3).

Full analysis saved to `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/aienvs/.context/compound-engineering/ce-review/20260425-032742-85562cea/agent-native.json`.</result>

      "description": "The schema marks 'concept_kinds', 'write_tool_owned', and 'progress' as required on the Capabilities object. The intent doc states capabilities{} is the additive extension point: new fields land there without bumping the protocol. But 'required' works both ways under JSON Schema 2020-12 — if a future boolean capability is added and marked required, old CLI implementations that don't know the field will emit objects that fail validation against newer schemas. More critically, third-party adapters in other languages that don't explicitly serialize false booleans will omit 'write_tool_owned' and 'progress', failing schema validation. Resolution: remove 'write_tool_owned' and 'progress' from the required array; only 'concept_kinds' warrants required. No new capability field should ever enter required."
    },

**Depth selected: Deep** (3.3K lines, wire-protocol attack surface with strong risk signals — framing parser, base64 decode, identity correlation, op dispatch).

---

**Summary of what I found attacking each area:**

**ADV-001 (high, 0.95)** is the sharpest finding. `bufio.Reader.ReadString` in the header loop has no per-line length cap. The frame-size ceiling in DefaultMaxFrameBytes only fires after all headers are read. A peer that never sends `\n` on a header line can exhaust heap memory before any cap applies. The two paths orthogonal to each other — ADV-001 is width (line length), ADV-002 is depth (line count).

**ADV-004 (medium, 0.88)** is the base64 CPU/memory pre-rejection window. A 16 MiB frame contains up to ~16 MiB of base64 which decodes to ~12 MiB before the 8 MiB cap check fires. The existing test `TestDecodeOp_RejectsOversizedDecodedPayload` verifies the post-decode check but does not close the pre-decode allocation window. The cap-before-decode guard is `ceil(8 MiB * 4/3) = ~10.7 MiB` on the wire content string length.

**ADV-007 (low, 0.92)** and **ADV-010 (low, 0.95)** are schema/runtime divergences: empty encoding string accepted as utf8 despite being schema-invalid; mode unconstrained to [0, 4095] in the runtime despite the schema constraint. Both become exploitable if PR 2 acts on ops before schema validation rather than after.

**ADV-008 (medium, 0.83)** is the `result: null` cascade: the pointer-to-RawMessage indirection in envelopeWire means `null` result values are classified as `hasResult=true`, leading PR 2 to unmarshal a null result into a zero-value `InitializeResult{}` and treat initialization as successful against a non-functioning adapter.

**ADV-005** documents a misleading comment that says fractional IDs are "accepted but truncated" — they are in fact rejected. The comment is a future-maintainer trap that could cause someone to "fix" the code to actually truncate, creating ID collision bugs in the correlator.</result>

Findings saved to `/Users/justinbeaudry/Projects/aienvs/.context/compound-engineering/ce-review/20260425-032742-85562cea/adversarial.json`.

---

**Depth selected: Deep** (3.3K lines, wire-protocol attack surface with strong risk signals — framing parser, base64 decode, identity correlation, op dispatch).

---

**Summary of what I found attacking each area:**

**ADV-001 (high, 0.95)** is the sharpest finding. `bufio.Reader.ReadString` in the header loop has no per-line length cap. The frame-size ceiling in DefaultMaxFrameBytes only fires after all headers are read. A peer that never sends `\n` on a header line can exhaust heap memory before any cap applies. The two paths orthogonal to each other — ADV-001 is width (line length), ADV-002 is depth (line count).

**ADV-004 (medium, 0.88)** is the base64 CPU/memory pre-rejection window. A 16 MiB frame contains up to ~16 MiB of base64 which decodes to ~12 MiB before the 8 MiB cap check fires. The existing test `TestDecodeOp_RejectsOversizedDecodedPayload` verifies the post-decode check but does not close the pre-decode allocation window. The cap-before-decode guard is `ceil(8 MiB * 4/3) = ~10.7 MiB` on the wire content string length.

**ADV-007 (low, 0.92)** and **ADV-010 (low, 0.95)** are schema/runtime divergences: empty encoding string accepted as utf8 despite being schema-invalid; mode unconstrained to [0, 4095] in the runtime despite the schema constraint. Both become exploitable if PR 2 acts on ops before schema validation rather than after.

**ADV-008 (medium, 0.83)** is the `result: null` cascade: the pointer-to-RawMessage indirection in envelopeWire means `null` result values are classified as `hasResult=true`, leading PR 2 to unmarshal a null result into a zero-value `InitializeResult{}` and treat initialization as successful against a non-functioning adapter.

**ADV-005** documents a misleading comment that says fractional IDs are "accepted but truncated" — they are in fact rejected. The comment is a future-maintainer trap that could cause someone to "fix" the code to actually truncate, creating ID collision bugs in the correlator.</result>

input.command:
git add -A && git commit -m "$(cat <<'EOF'
fix: unit 8 pr1 -- ce-review autofix round 1

Address findings from ce:review against the wire-protocol layer.

Correctness fixes:
- ParseMessage now distinguishes "key absent" from "key present with
  null value" by decoding into a generic key map first. Restores
  JSON-RPC 2.0 spec compliance for two mandated wire shapes:
  (1) {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":null,"error":...} -- error responses to
  requests whose id couldn't be determined (§5.1).
  (2) {"jsonrpc":"2.0","id":1,"result":null} -- void return values.
  Both were rejected as ErrInvalidEnvelope before.
- Response.MarshalJSON tightens hasResult guard from `!= nil` to
  `len(...) > 0`. A non-nil zero-length json.RawMessage is now treated
  as absent rather than producing malformed wire bytes.
- marshalEnvelope suppresses _meta when the payload equals the literal
  "null", matching `omitempty` semantics on the typed struct fields.
  Fixes a divergence where Meta = json.RawMessage("null") would emit
  "_meta":null while a typed nil Meta would emit nothing.

Defense-in-depth:
- OpWriteFile.MarshalJSON and OpWriteToolOwned.MarshalJSON now enforce
  MaxOpPayloadBytes. Direct struct construction bypassing
  NewOpWriteFile can no longer smuggle oversized payloads onto the
  wire.
- Extracted encodeOpContent helper, symmetric with decodeOpContent.

Schema fixes:
- initialize.json: Capabilities.required reduced to just concept_kinds.
  write_tool_owned and progress are booleans whose absence/false-ness
  must not break additive extension; the parent plan's "freeze the
  wire frame, grow capabilities" rule depends on this.
- initialized.json: include _meta in properties to keep the additive
  extension point consistent across all method schemas.

Maintainability:
- Removed dead `jsonrpc bool` field from envelopeFields; the value was
  always true at every call site.
- Renamed schema_parity_test.go's `propsPath` -> `schemaNavPath` to
  reflect that it is a full doc-traversal path, not a path to a
  single properties key.
- Fixed misleading comment claiming fractional IDs are "truncated"
  when in fact json.Number.Int64() rejects them.
- Doc-link InitializeResult.ProtocolVersion to
  ErrorClassAdapterProtocolMismatch.

Tests added:
- ParseMessage rejects response without id (T-04).
- ParseMessage rejects envelope with no method/result/error (T-05).
- ParseMessage accepts null-id error response.
- ParseMessage accepts null-result void response.
- Response.MarshalJSON rejects zero-length non-nil RawMessage.
- Request marshaling suppresses literal-null _meta.
- ToolOwnedKind stable wire values for json-pointer / toml-path /
  markdown-section (T-08).
- WarningStatus stable wire values for degraded / partial (T-09).
- Direct OpWriteFile / OpWriteToolOwned construction at oversized
  content fails MarshalOp.

Plan doc: corrected docs/solutions/ paths to reflect the real
category-organized layout (best-practices/, workflow-issues/).

Run artifact: .context/compound-engineering/ce-review/20260425-032742-85562cea/

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
EOF
)"

input.content:
# ce-review run 20260425-032742-85562cea

**Plan:** [docs/plans/2026-04-25-001-feat-unit-8-pr1-wire-protocol-plan.md](../../../../docs/plans/2026-04-25-001-feat-unit-8-pr1-wire-protocol-plan.md)
**Branch:** `feat/unit-8-pr1-wire-protocol`
**Base:** `5f9c964` (origin/main)
**Mode:** autofix
**Diff:** 18 files, +3293/-0 (4 commits before review; 1 autofix commit added)
**Reviewers run:** correctness, testing, maintainability, project-standards, agent-native, learnings-researcher, api-contract, adversarial

## Verdict

**Ready with fixes** — core wire-protocol layer is correct after autofix round 1. Several gated/manual findings remain for follow-up; they are PR-2-or-later concerns, not PR-1 blockers.

## Applied Fixes (round 1, safe_auto)

Committed in `710ba3f fix: unit 8 pr1 -- ce-review autofix round 1`.

| # | Finding | File | Outcome |
|---|---------|------|---------|
| C-001 | ParseMessage rejects spec-compliant null-id error responses | `internal/adapter/contract/jsonrpc.go` | Rewrote ParseMessage to use generic key map; null-id error responses now classify as Response with `IsNull()`. |
| C-002 | ParseMessage rejects valid result:null responses | same | Same fix as C-001. |
| ADV-008 | result:null cascade — opposite claim from C-002, but identical fix | same | Resolved together with C-001/C-002. |
| C-003 | `Response.MarshalJSON` produces malformed JSON for zero-length non-nil RawMessage | same | Tightened guard to `len(...) > 0`. |
| C-004 | `marshalEnvelope` emits `_meta:null` for `RawMessage("null")` | same | Now suppressed via `bytes.Equal(f.meta, []byte("null"))` check. |
| C-005 | `OpWriteFile.MarshalJSON` doesn't enforce payload cap | `internal/adapter/contract/protocol.go` | Added cap check at the top of MarshalJSON for both `OpWriteFile` and `OpWriteToolOwned`. |
| M-01 | Dead `jsonrpc bool` field in envelopeFields | `internal/adapter/contract/jsonrpc.go` | Removed; jsonrpc emission is now unconditional. |
| M-02 | Encode-side duplication between OpWriteFile and OpWriteToolOwned | `internal/adapter/contract/protocol.go` | Extracted `encodeOpContent` helper symmetric with `decodeOpContent`. |
| M-05 | `propsPath` field name understates what the value is | `internal/adapter/contract/schema_parity_test.go` | Renamed to `schemaNavPath`. |
| ADV-005 | Misleading comment claims fractional IDs are "truncated" | `internal/adapter/contract/jsonrpc.go` | Comment now states they are rejected. |
| O1 | No doc link from `ProtocolVersion` to `ErrorClassAdapterProtocolMismatch` | `internal/adapter/contract/protocol.go` | Doc comment added. |
| AC-001 | Schema `capabilities.required` includes `write_tool_owned` and `progress` | `internal/adapter/contract/schema/initialize.json` | Reduced required to just `concept_kinds`; preserves additive extension promise. |
| AC-008 | `initialized.json` has `additionalProperties:false` but no `_meta` | `internal/adapter/contract/schema/initialized.json` | `_meta` added to properties. |
| PSR-001 | Plan doc references broken `docs/solutions/` paths | `docs/plans/2026-04-25-001-feat-unit-8-pr1-wire-protocol-plan.md` | Updated to actual category-organized paths. |
| T-04 | No test for "response missing id" branch | `internal/adapter/contract/jsonrpc_test.go` | Added. |
| T-05 | No test for "no method/result/error" branch | same | Added. |
| T-08 | No stable-value test for ToolOwnedKind | `internal/adapter/contract/protocol_test.go` | Added. |
| T-09 | No stable-value test for WarningStatus | same | Added. |
| (new) | No tests for null-id / null-result / zero-length-result / null-meta | `internal/adapter/contract/jsonrpc_test.go` | Added. |
| (new) | No test for marshal-side payload cap on direct OpWriteFile/OpWriteToolOwned construction | `internal/adapter/contract/protocol_test.go` | Added. |

## Residual Actionable Work (gated_auto / manual)

Not auto-applied. These either change wire behavior, require design decisions, or are PR-2-or-later concerns that PR 1 should not pre-empt.

### P1 — wire-contract or DOS hardening

| # | Finding | Action | Owner |
|---|---------|--------|-------|
| ADV-001 | Unbounded header-line length in `FrameReader.Read` allows memory exhaustion before frame-size cap fires (DOS vector) | Wrap header reader with `io.LimitReader` at e.g. 8 KiB per line; add test for oversized header line. | downstream-resolver |
| AC-002 | `OpWarning.OpPath()` returns empty string; flows into `OpRecord.Path` with no schema discrimination | Decide: separate `WarningRecord` in `EmitResult`, or document the "" semantic in spec + schema. PR 2 declared-outputs gate must special-case it either way. | downstream-resolver (decision) |
| AC-003 | `emit.json` does not `$ref` the `op_*.json` schemas; op stream is opaque to third-party validators | Add `$defs.op` discriminated union in `emit.json` referencing each op schema; or build a separate `ops.json` index schema. PR 3 conformance harness needs this. | downstream-resolver (PR 2 or 3) |
| AC-005 / O2 | `DeclaredOutput.JSONPath` (JSONPath syntax `$.foo`) collides with `ToolOwnedKindJSONPointer` (RFC 6901 `/foo`) | Rename `DeclaredOutput.JSONPath` to `JSONPointer` (wire `json_pointer`) and align with the op locator standard. **Wire-breaking; do before PR 3 spec freeze.** | downstream-resolver (pre-freeze) |
| AC-006 / ADV-007 | `decodeOpContent` accepts empty `encoding` string as utf8, but schemas mark `encoding` as required → schema-vs-runtime divergence | Choose: tighten Go decoder to reject empty encoding (matches schema, ADV-007 pref), or relax schema to make encoding optional with utf8 default. | downstream-resolver (decision pre-freeze) |

### P2 — defensive / design

| # | Finding | Action | Owner |
|---|---------|--------|-------|
| ADV-002 | Unbounded header count allows memory exhaustion via 1M short unknown header lines | Add header-count ceiling (e.g., 32) before final blank line. | downstream-resolver |
| ADV-004 | `base64.DecodeString` allocates ~12 MiB before the 8 MiB cap check | Reject base64 strings longer than `ceil(MaxOpPayloadBytes * 4/3) ≈ 10.7 MiB` before calling DecodeString. | downstream-resolver |
| ADV-006 | `IDCorrelator.pending` map grows without bound under hung adapter | Add `SetMaxPending(n)` API or expose `PendingCount()` so PR 2 can shed load. | downstream-resolver (PR 2) |
| AC-007 | `MaxOpPayloadBytes` (8 MiB) not represented in any schema | Add `description` annotation referencing the constant; full `maxLength` requires base64-aware constraint. | downstream-resolver |

### P3 — low-impact polish

| # | Finding | Action | Owner |
|---|---------|--------|-------|
| W1 | `OpWarning.Note` is prose-only, no machine-readable sub-code | Optional: add `OpWarning.Code` (string) sub-classifier. Defer until concrete warning variants need to disambiguate. | downstream-resolver |
| W2 | No `ErrorClassAdapterInvalidIR` for adapter-side semantic IR rejection | Optional: add before PR 3 spec freeze if adapters need a stable class. | downstream-resolver (pre-freeze) |
| O3 | `ErrorData` has no `retryable` boolean | Optional: add for agent retry policy uniformity. | downstream-resolver |
| ADV-009 | Duplicate JSON keys silently last-wins (Go stdlib limitation) | Document; PR 2 can use `json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields` or a strict-mode wrapper if hostile peers are a real threat. | advisory |
| ADV-010 | `mode` uint32 unchecked in OpWriteFile/OpMkdir; schema caps at 4095 but Go runtime doesn't | Add `mode > 0o7777` validation in op constructors. | downstream-resolver |
| ADV-011 | Trailing bytes after JSON object in `ParseMessage` silently ignored | Add `dec.More()` check to surface anomaly. Low impact; advisory. | advisory |
| AC-009 | `Encoding` type exported despite "wire-only/internal" comment | Either unexport (PR 1 is the cheapest moment) or document why exported. | downstream-resolver |
| ADV-003 | FrameReader stream desync after partial-read error | Document the recovery contract; PR 2 must handle. | advisory (PR 2) |
| M-03 | `MarshalOp` is a one-line alias over `json.Marshal` | Either inline at call sites or grow into a real validator. Defer until PR 2 reveals which. | advisory |
| M-04 | `SchemaFS` exported with no consumers yet | Could unexport until PR 3 adds adapterkit. Low impact. | advisory |
| PSR-002 | Framing functions take no `context.Context` | Debatable — project precedent (`internal/trust/store.go`) omits context for stream-level byte I/O. PR 2 should establish the convention for runtime. | advisory |

### Pre-existing / out of scope

None — this is a greenfield package.

## Learnings & Past Solutions

Two relevant learnings, both already followed:

- **`docs/solutions/best-practices/go-windows-cross-platform-pitfalls-2026-04-24.md`** — Go cross-platform pitfalls. Schema embedding via `embed.FS` uses forward slashes mandated by `io/fs.FS`; correctly used. No `runtime.GOOS` skips in this PR's tests.
- **`docs/solutions/workflow-issues/spec-impl-drift-at-pr-review-2026-04-25.md`** — Schema/types drift caught at PR review. The hand-rolled parity test in `schema_parity_test.go` is the recommended pattern; one residual gap (RR-01: omitempty-vs-required parity) is documented for PR 3 to tighten.

## Agent-Native Gaps

- **W1** (above): `OpWarning.Note` is prose-only — no sub-code disambiguation if a `concept_id + status` pair has multiple variant reasons. Document or add a code field.
- **W2**: No `ErrorClassAdapterInvalidIR` for emit-time IR rejection. Agents currently must parse `Error.Message` strings to distinguish process failures from semantic IR rejection.

## Coverage

- 8 of 8 reviewers completed.
- 0 reviewers timed out or failed.
- Suppressed: 0 findings below 0.60 confidence.
- Untracked files excluded: none.
- `go vet ./...`, `go test -race ./...`, and `golangci-lint run ./...` all green at HEAD.

| W1 | `OpWarning.Note` is prose-only, no machine-readable sub-code | Optional: add `OpWarning.Code` (string) sub-classifier. Defer until concrete warning variants need to disambiguate. | downstream-resolver |
| W2 | No `ErrorClassAdapterInvalidIR` for adapter-side semantic IR rejection | Optional: add before PR 3 spec freeze if adapters need a stable class. | downstream-resolver (pre-freeze) |
| O3 | `ErrorData` has no `retryable` boolean | Optional: add for agent retry policy uniformity. | downstream-resolver |
| ADV-009 | Duplicate JSON keys silently last-wins (Go stdlib limitation) | Document; PR 2 can use `json.Decoder.DisallowUnknownFields` or a strict-mode wrapper if hostile peers are a real threat. | advisory |
| ADV-010 | `mode` uint32 unchecked in OpWriteFile/OpMkdir; schema caps at 4095 but Go runtime doesn't | Add `mode > 0o7777` validation in op constructors. | downstream-resolver |
| ADV-011 | Trailing bytes after JSON object in `ParseMessage` silently ignored | Add `dec.More()` check to surface anomaly. Low impact; advisory. | advisory |
| AC-009 | `Encoding` type exported despite "wire-only/internal" comment | Either unexport (PR 1 is the cheapest moment) or document why exported. | downstream-resolver |
| ADV-003 | FrameReader stream desync after partial-read error | Document the recovery contract; PR 2 must handle. | advisory (PR 2) |
| M-03 | `MarshalOp` is a one-line alias over `json.Marshal` | Either inline at call sites or grow into a real validator. Defer until PR 2 reveals which. | advisory |
| M-04 | `SchemaFS` exported with no consumers yet | Could unexport until PR 3 adds adapterkit. Low impact. | advisory |
| PSR-002 | Framing functions take no `context.Context` | Debatable — project precedent (`internal/trust/store.go`) omits context for stream-level byte I/O. PR 2 should establish the convention for runtime. | advisory |

### Pre-existing / out of scope

None — this is a greenfield package.

## Learnings & Past Solutions

Two relevant learnings, both already followed:

- **`docs/solutions/best-practices/go-windows-cross-platform-pitfalls-2026-04-24.md`** — Go cross-platform pitfalls. Schema embedding via `embed.FS` uses forward slashes mandated by `io/fs.FS`; correctly used. No `runtime.GOOS` skips in this PR's tests.
- **`docs/solutions/workflow-issues/spec-impl-drift-at-pr-review-2026-04-25.md`** — Schema/types drift caught at PR review. The hand-rolled parity test in `schema_parity_test.go` is the recommended pattern; one residual gap (RR-01: omitempty-vs-required parity) is documented for PR 3 to tighten.

## Agent-Native Gaps

- **W1** (above): `OpWarning.Note` is prose-only — no sub-code disambiguation if a `concept_id + status` pair has multiple variant reasons. Document or add a code field.
- **W2**: No `ErrorClassAdapterInvalidIR` for emit-time IR rejection. Agents currently must parse `Error.Message` strings to distinguish process failures from semantic IR rejection.

## Coverage

- 8 of 8 reviewers completed.
- 0 reviewers timed out or failed.
- Suppressed: 0 findings below 0.60 confidence.
- Untracked files excluded: none.
- `go vet ./...`, `go test -race ./...`, and `golangci-lint run ./...` all green at HEAD.

Base directory for this skill: /Users/justinbeaudry/.claude/plugins/cache/compound-engineering-plugin/compound-engineering/2.67.0/skills/todo-resolve

Resolve approved todos using parallel processing, document lessons learned, then clean up.

Only `ready` todos are resolved. `pending` todos are skipped — they haven't been triaged yet. If pending todos exist, list them at the end so the user knows what was left behind.

## Workflow

### 1. Analyze

Scan `.context/compound-engineering/todos/*.md` and legacy `todos/*.md`. Partition by status:

- **`ready`** (status field or `-ready-` in filename): resolve these.
- **`pending`**: skip. Report them at the end.
- **`complete`**: ignore, already done.

If a specific todo ID or pattern was passed as an argument, filter to matching todos only (still must be `ready`).

Residual actionable work from `ce:review mode:autofix` after its `safe_auto` pass will already be `ready`.

Skip any todo that recommends deleting, removing, or gitignoring files in `docs/brainstorms/`, `docs/plans/`, or `docs/solutions/` — these are intentional pipeline artifacts.

### 2. Plan

Create a task list grouped by type (e.g., `TaskCreate` in Claude Code, `update_plan` in Codex). Analyze dependencies -- items that others depend on run first. Output a mermaid diagram showing execution order and parallelism.

### 3. Implement (PARALLEL)

Spawn a `compound-engineering:workflow:pr-comment-resolver` agent per item. Prefer parallel; fall back to sequential respecting dependency order.

**Batching:** 1-4 items: direct parallel returns. 5+ items: batches of 4, each returning only a short status summary (todo handled, files changed, tests run/skipped, blockers).

For large sets, use a scratch directory at `.context/compound-engineering/todo-resolve/<run-id>/` for per-resolver artifacts. Return only completion summaries to parent.

### 4. Commit & Resolve

Commit changes, mark todos resolved, push to remote.

GATE: STOP. Verify todos resolved and changes committed before proceeding.

### 5. Compound on Lessons Learned

Load the `ce:compound` skill to document what was learned. Todo resolutions often surface patterns and architectural insights worth capturing.

GATE: STOP. Verify the compound skill produced a solution document in `docs/solutions/`. If none (user declined or no learnings), continue.

### 6. Clean Up

Delete completed/resolved todo files from both paths. If a scratch directory was created at `.context/compound-engineering/todo-resolve/<run-id>/`, delete it (unless user asked to inspect).

```
Todos resolved: [count]
Pending (skipped): [count, or "none"]
Lessons documented: [path to solution doc, or "skipped"]
Todos cleaned up: [count deleted]
```

If pending todos were skipped, list them:

```
Skipped pending todos (run /todo-triage to approve):
  - 003-pending-p2-missing-index.md
  - 005-pending-p3-rename-variable.md
```

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/justinbeaudry/.claude/plugins/cache/compound-engineering-plugin/compound-engineering/2.67.0/skills/test-browser

# Browser Test Skill

Run end-to-end browser tests on pages affected by a PR or branch changes using the `agent-browser` CLI.

## Use `agent-browser` Only For Browser Automation

This workflow uses the `agent-browser` CLI exclusively. Do not use any alternative browser automation system, browser MCP integration, or built-in browser-control tool. If the platform offers multiple ways to control a browser, always choose `agent-browser`.

Use `agent-browser` for: opening pages, clicking elements, filling forms, taking screenshots, and scraping rendered content.

Platform-specific hints:
- In Claude Code, do not use Chrome MCP tools (`mcp__claude-in-chrome__*`).
- In Codex, do not substitute unrelated browsing tools.

## Prerequisites

- Local development server running (e.g., `bin/dev`, `rails server`, `npm run dev`)
- `agent-browser` CLI installed (see Setup below)
- Git repository with changes to test

## Setup

Check whether `agent-browser` is installed:

```bash
command -v agent-browser >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "Installed" || echo "NOT INSTALLED"
```

If not installed, inform the user: "`agent-browser` is not installed. Run `/ce-setup` to install required dependencies." Then stop — this skill cannot function without agent-browser.

## Workflow

### 1. Verify Installation

Before starting, verify `agent-browser` is available:

```bash
command -v agent-browser >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo "Ready" || echo "NOT INSTALLED"
```

If not installed, inform the user: "`agent-browser` is not installed. Run `/ce-setup` to install required dependencies." Then stop.

### 2. Ask Browser Mode

Ask the user whether to run headed or headless (using the platform's question tool — e.g., `AskUserQuestion` in Claude Code, `request_user_input` in Codex, `ask_user` in Gemini — or present options and wait for a reply):

```
Do you want to watch the browser tests run?

1. Headed (watch) - Opens visible browser window so you can see tests run
2. Headless (faster) - Runs in background, faster but invisible
```

Store the choice and use the `--headed` flag when the user selects option 1.

### 3. Determine Test Scope

**If PR number provided:**
```bash
gh pr view [number] --json files -q '.files[].path'
```

**If 'current' or empty:**
```bash
git diff --name-only main...HEAD
```

**If branch name provided:**
```bash
git diff --name-only main...[branch]
```

### 4. Map Files to Routes

Map changed files to testable routes:

| File Pattern | Route(s) |
|-------------|----------|
| `app/views/users/*` | `/users`, `/users/:id`, `/users/new` |
| `app/controllers/settings_controller.rb` | `/settings` |
| `app/javascript/controllers/*_controller.js` | Pages using that Stimulus controller |
| `app/components/*_component.rb` | Pages rendering that component |
| `app/views/layouts/*` | All pages (test homepage at minimum) |
| `app/assets/stylesheets/*` | Visual regression on key pages |
| `app/helpers/*_helper.rb` | Pages using that helper |
| `src/app/*` (Next.js) | Corresponding routes |
| `src/components/*` | Pages using those components |

Build a list of URLs to test based on the mapping.

### 5. Detect Dev Server Port

Determine the dev server port using this priority:

1. **Explicit argument** — if the user passed `--port 5000`, use that directly
2. **Project instructions** — check `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md`, or other instruction files for port references
3. **package.json** — check dev/start scripts for `--port` flags
4. **Environment files** — check `.env`, `.env.local`, `.env.development` for `PORT=`
5. **Default** — fall back to `3000`

```bash
PORT="${EXPLICIT_PORT:-}"
if [ -z "$PORT" ]; then
  PORT=$(grep -Eio '(port\s*[:=]\s*|localhost:)([0-9]{4,5})' AGENTS.md 2>/dev/null | grep -Eo '[0-9]{4,5}' | head -1)
  if [ -z "$PORT" ]; then
    PORT=$(grep -Eio '(port\s*[:=]\s*|localhost:)([0-9]{4,5})' CLAUDE.md 2>/dev/null | grep -Eo '[0-9]{4,5}' | head -1)
  fi
fi
if [ -z "$PORT" ]; then
  PORT=$(grep -Eo '\-\-port[= ]+[0-9]{4,5}' package.json 2>/dev/null | grep -Eo '[0-9]{4,5}' | head -1)
fi
if [ -z "$PORT" ]; then
  PORT=$(grep -h '^PORT=' .env .env.local .env.development 2>/dev/null | tail -1 | cut -d= -f2)
fi
PORT="${PORT:-3000}"
echo "Using dev server port: $PORT"
```

### 6. Verify Server is Running

```bash
agent-browser open http://localhost:${PORT}
agent-browser snapshot -i
```

If the server is not running, inform the user:

```
Server not running on port ${PORT}

Please start your development server:
- Rails: `bin/dev` or `rails server`
- Node/Next.js: `npm run dev`
- Custom port: run this skill again with `--port <your-port>`

Then re-run this skill.
```

### 7. Test Each Affected Page

For each affected route:

**Navigate and capture snapshot:**
```bash
agent-browser open "http://localhost:${PORT}/[route]"
agent-browser snapshot -i
```

**For headed mode:**
```bash
agent-browser --headed open "http://localhost:${PORT}/[route]"
agent-browser --headed snapshot -i
```

**Verify key elements:**
- Use `agent-browser snapshot -i` to get interactive elements with refs
- Page title/heading present
- Primary content rendered
- No error messages visible
- Forms have expected fields

**Test critical interactions:**
```bash
agent-browser click @e1
agent-browser snapshot -i
```

**Take screenshots:**
```bash
agent-browser screenshot page-name.png
agent-browser screenshot --full page-name-full.png
```

### 8. Human Verification (When Required)

Pause for human input when testing touches flows that require external interaction:

| Flow Type | What to Ask |
|-----------|-------------|
| OAuth | "Please sign in with [provider] and confirm it works" |
| Email | "Check your inbox for the test email and confirm receipt" |
| Payments | "Complete a test purchase in sandbox mode" |
| SMS | "Verify you received the SMS code" |
| External APIs | "Confirm the [service] integration is working" |

Ask the user (using the platform's question tool, or present numbered options and wait):

```
Human Verification Needed

This test touches [flow type]. Please:
1. [Action to take]
2. [What to verify]

Did it work correctly?
1. Yes - continue testing
2. No - describe the issue
```

### 9. Handle Failures

When a test fails:

1. **Document the failure:**
   - Screenshot the error state: `agent-browser screenshot error.png`
   - Note the exact reproduction steps

2. **Ask the user how to proceed:**

   ```
   Test Failed: [route]

   Issue: [description]
   Console errors: [if any]

   How to proceed?
   1. Fix now - I'll help debug and fix
   2. Create todo - Add a todo for later (using the todo-create skill)
   3. Skip - Continue testing other pages
   ```

3. **If "Fix now":** investigate, propose a fix, apply, re-run the failing test
4. **If "Create todo":** load the `todo-create` skill and create a todo with priority p1 and description `browser-test-{description}`, continue
5. **If "Skip":** log as skipped, continue

### 10. Test Summary

After all tests complete, present a summary:

```markdown
## Browser Test Results

**Test Scope:** PR #[number] / [branch name]
**Server:** http://localhost:${PORT}

### Pages Tested: [count]

| Route | Status | Notes |
|-------|--------|-------|
| `/users` | Pass | |
| `/settings` | Pass | |
| `/dashboard` | Fail | Console error: [msg] |
| `/checkout` | Skip | Requires payment credentials |

### Console Errors: [count]
- [List any errors found]

### Human Verifications: [count]
- OAuth flow: Confirmed
- Email delivery: Confirmed

### Failures: [count]
- `/dashboard` - [issue description]

### Created Todos: [count]
- `005-pending-p1-browser-test-dashboard-error.md`

### Result: [PASS / FAIL / PARTIAL]
```

## Quick Usage Examples

```bash
# Test current branch changes (auto-detects port)
/test-browser

# Test specific PR
/test-browser 847

# Test specific branch
/test-browser feature/new-dashboard

# Test on a specific port
/test-browser --port 5000
```

## agent-browser CLI Reference

Run `agent-browser --help` for all commands.

Key commands:

```bash
# Navigation
agent-browser open <url>           # Navigate to URL
agent-browser back                 # Go back
agent-browser close                # Close browser

# Snapshots (get element refs)
agent-browser snapshot -i          # Interactive elements with refs (@e1, @e2, etc.)
agent-browser snapshot -i --json   # JSON output

# Interactions (use refs from snapshot)
agent-browser click @e1            # Click element
agent-browser fill @e1 "text"      # Fill input
agent-browser type @e1 "text"      # Type without clearing
agent-browser press Enter          # Press key

# Screenshots
agent-browser screenshot out.png       # Viewport screenshot
agent-browser screenshot --full out.png # Full page screenshot

# Headed mode (visible browser)
agent-browser --headed open <url>      # Open with visible browser
agent-browser --headed click @e1       # Click in visible browser

# Wait
agent-browser wait @e1             # Wait for element
agent-browser wait 2000            # Wait milliseconds
```
